title: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel as Second-Line Therapy for Large B-Cell Lymphoma (ZUMA-7)
authors_year: Locke FL, Miklos DB, Jacobson CA, et al. 2022
design: Phase 3, randomized (1:1), open-label, international superiority trial. Axi-cel (autologous anti-CD19 CAR-T) vs standard of care (platinum-based salvage chemoimmunotherapy followed by high-dose therapy + ASCT in responders). Randomization stratified by response to first-line therapy (primary refractory vs relapse ≤6 mo vs relapse >6-≤12 mo) and second-line age-adjusted IPI. Primary endpoint: event-free survival by blinded central review. Note: the local full-text file is the trial protocol + statistical analysis plan supplement, which grounds design and eligibility verbatim; headline efficacy numbers below are the published ZUMA-7 primary results.
population: Adults with R/R DLBCL after first-line rituximab + anthracycline-based chemoimmunotherapy; disease either primary-refractory or relapsed within 12 months of initiating first-line therapy. Transplant-intended population (intent to proceed to HDT/ASCT if second-line response). N=359 randomized (axi-cel 180 vs SOC 179).
key_eligibility: INCLUDES the highest-risk 2L population: primary-refractory disease AND early relapse (≤12 months) — the opposite of L-MIND, which excludes primary-refractory. This is the population where 2L CAR-T is indicated.
Refractory defined as no complete remission to first-line therapy (PD as best response, or SD after ≥4 cycles); patients merely intolerant to first-line therapy were excluded.
Relapsed disease defined as CR to first-line followed by biopsy-proven relapse ≤12 months of initiating first-line therapy.
Required adequate first-line therapy: anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody (unless CD20-negative) AND an anthracycline-containing regimen.
Intent to proceed to HDT and ASCT if response to second-line therapy (i.e., otherwise transplant-eligible/intended patients).
Exclusion: history of autologous or allogeneic stem cell transplant.
outcomes: Published ZUMA-7 primary results: median EFS 8.3 vs 2.0 months, HR 0.40 (95% CI 0.31-0.51), P<0.001; 24-month EFS 41% vs 16%. ORR 83% vs 50%; CR 65% vs 32%. Only ~35% of SOC arm proceeded to ASCT. Axi-cel grade ≥3 CRS 6%, grade ≥3 neurologic events 21%. Establishes 2L axi-cel superior to salvage chemo/ASCT for primary-refractory/early-relapse LBCL.
grounds_rubric_claims: 2L CAR-T (axi-cel) is superior to salvage chemotherapy + ASCT for primary-refractory or early-relapse (<12 mo) LBCL on EFS
Primary-refractory / early-relapse (<12 mo) LBCL is an indication for 2L CAR-T rather than salvage/ASCT
2L axi-cel eligibility requires prior anti-CD20 + anthracycline first-line therapy and transplant intent

verbatim_quotes.quote: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study Evaluating the Efficacy of Axicabtagene Ciloleucel versus Standard of Care Therapy in Subjects with Relapsed/Refractory Diffuse Large B Cell Lymphoma (ZUMA-7)
verbatim_quotes.supports: Design: head-to-head randomized comparison of 2L axi-cel vs standard salvage-of-care in R/R DLBCL

verbatim_quotes.quote: Randomization will be stratified by response to first-line therapy (primary refractory, vs relapse ≤ 6 months of initiating first-line therapy vs relapse > 6 and ≤ 12 months of initiating first-line therapy)
verbatim_quotes.supports: Population = primary-refractory or relapse within 12 months (the early-relapse/refractory group where 2L CAR-T applies)

verbatim_quotes.quote: Refractory disease defined as no complete remission to first-line therapy; subjects who are intolerant to first-line therapy are excluded
verbatim_quotes.supports: Key eligibility definition of primary-refractory disease grounding 2L CAR-T indication

verbatim_quotes.quote: Intent to proceed to HDT and ASCT if response to second-line therapy
verbatim_quotes.supports: Transplant-intended population — axi-cel compared directly against the salvage-chemo-then-ASCT pathway

caveats: Local file is the ZUMA-7 protocol + SAP supplement (not the results paper); it grounds design, stratification, and eligibility verbatim. The efficacy numbers (EFS HR 0.40, median 8.3 vs 2.0 mo, ORR 83%/CR 65%) are the published primary results and should be cited to the NEJM article body, not to the protocol text.
